import {Meta, Typeset, Unstyled, Story, Source} from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import '../../card/card';
import '../../button/button';
import bannerUrl from './lift-banner-image.jpg';

<Meta title="Overview/Introduction" />

<div className="lift-intro">

    <div className="lift-intro-banner">
    <div className="lift-intro-banner-inner">
        <div className="lift-intro-banner-content atp-color-inverse">
            <h1 className="lift-heading atp-color-inverse" style={{fontSize: '100px', lineHeight: '1em', margin: '0'}}>Lift</h1>

            ATPCO’s new design system – a user experience feature that unites and accelerates design and engineering across our web offerings.

            <a href="/?path=/docs/overview-getting-started--docs">
                <atp-button label="Get started" appearance="outline" color="primary" size="large"></atp-button>
            </a>
        </div>
        </div>
    </div>

    <div className="lift-intro-content">

        <atp-card style={{marginBottom: 'var(--atp-space-xl)'}}>
            <atp-card-header slot="header">
            <h2>What is a design system?</h2>
            </atp-card-header>

            A design system is a set of user interface building blocks and standards that promote a consistent user experience.
            A design system includes both Figma components (for designers) and code components (for engineers), so it unites and synchronizes the disciplines, creating a common vocabulary for both.

            Think of a design system as the set of Lego bricks that teams use to build web applications and websites.
            The design system acts as an accelerator: teams use these prebuilt, ready-to-go blocks to build new experiences quickly, easily, and at scale.
            Moreover, since the blocks use common visuals and branding, every project that uses the design system easily shares that look.
            This consistent visual experience draws a connection between ATPCO’s various applications, thus strengthening our brand identity and our credibility as a technology provider.

            By using a design system, we gain advantages of scale _and_ gain visual consistency!

        </atp-card>

        <atp-card>
            <atp-card-header slot="header">
                <h2>The name Lift</h2>
            </atp-card-header>

            We chose a name that evokes a spirit of upward momentum, and of elevating both people and experiences.
            Lift raises ATPCO’s product teams by enabling faster innovation and more seamless collaboration across disciplines.
            For customers, it creates an ecosystem that removes friction and helps them soar.

            So we wanted a name that was accessible yet expressed this aspirational tone. “Lift” captures our mission without sounding intimidating or technical.

        </atp-card>

        <atp-card>
            <atp-card-header slot="header">
                <h2>Why a new design system?</h2>
            </atp-card-header>

            Our older design system, called Hub, wasn’t sufficiently flexible to meet our modern needs.
            It only worked in Angular, it didn’t support modern web standards, and it tied us to older visuals.

            We wanted to learn from those lessons, so we could build something flexible and cross-framework that will meet ATPCO’s future needs.

        </atp-card>

        <div className="lift-intro-footer">

            <h2> Ready to explore Lift?</h2>

            <div className="lift-intro-footer-slogan">
            Learn how to use Lift's components, patterns, and principles to build modern, cohesive ATPCO experiences.
            </div>

            <a href="/?path=/docs/overview-getting-started--docs">
                <atp-button label="Get started" appearance="fill" color="primary"></atp-button>
            </a>
        </div>

    </div>

</div>
